digest.top_line: BART: adjuvant pelvic RT post-cystectomy cuts 2yr locoregional failure (LRFFS 87.1% vs 76.0%, HR 0.43, p=0.04); no OS benefit (HR 0.78, ns).
digest.tldr: Bladder carried the day. BART, the first phase III of adjuvant pelvic RT after radical cystectomy, roughly halves locoregional failure (8% vs 26%) in high-risk MIBC, benefit concentrating in N+/T3+ pts. OS unchanged, and the trial predates adjuvant nivolumab, so the RT+IO question stays open.

sites.intro: First randomised evidence for adjuvant RT after radical cystectomy, though underpowered and analyzed per-protocol.

studies.name: BART
studies.tldr: 2yr locoregional FFS 87.1% vs 76.0% with adjuvant RT post-cystectomy, HR 0.43 (0.20-0.96), p=0.04; OS not improved (HR 0.78, ns).

details.text: 📊 2yr outcomes, adjuvant RT vs observation (LRFFS primary, rest secondary)

details: 📊 LR recurrence 8% (RT) vs 26% (obs), p=0.006; no isolated locoregional recurrences with RT
🔍 Phase III RCT, N=153 (RT 77 / obs 76), high-risk (T3-4, N1-3, R+) MIBC post-cystectomy + chemo, 2016-2024
🔍 RT: cystectomy bed + pelvic nodes (common/internal/external iliac, presacral, obturator), 50.4Gy/28fx, stoma/bowel-sparing IMRT, daily IGRT

details.text: 📊 2yr LRFFS benefit concentrates in high-risk subsets (and per-protocol)

details: ⚠️ Primary analysis per-protocol, not ITT: 14 RT-assigned pts (refused 8, progression 4, unfeasible 2) analyzed in observation arm
⚠️ Underpowered: trial missed its accrual target
⚠️ No immunotherapy given; predates adjuvant nivolumab as SOC, so doesn't address the RT+IO question

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase III, prespecified primary endpoint (2yr LRFFS) met, contesting the observation-only standard for high-risk pts. Per-protocol analysis and missed accrual are caveats, not enough to discount the divergence.
verdict.audience: Urothelial MIBC, high-risk (≥pT3 / N+ / R+), post-cystectomy + chemo, M0

studies.significance: Offer adjuvant pelvic RT (50.4Gy/28fx, cystectomy bed plus iliac/presacral/obturator nodes) to ≥pT3, N+, or margin-positive pts, where benefit concentrates (N+ HR 0.22) and isolated locoregional failure was eliminated. Late grade 3+ toxicity matched observation (8.4% vs 10.5%, p=0.60), answering the late-morbidity fear. Read it as local control: OS was unchanged.
open_questions: OS benefit unconfirmed pending MERCY individual-patient-data meta-analysis
Adjuvant RT plus immunotherapy combinations with non-overlapping toxicity
Generalizability without immunotherapy and in younger cohort (median age 57)

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Concurrent Immunotherapy and Radiotherapy for the Treatment of Bladder Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: The primary objective of this Phase I study is to establish the safety of adjuvant concurrent immunotherapy and radiation therapy for urothelial bladder cancer.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Pure or mixed variant urothelial carcinoma

  o Allowable mixed variant subtypes include:
  * squamous differentiation
  * glandular differentiation
  * nested pattern
  * microcystic
  * micropapillary
  * lymphoepthelioma-like
  * plasmacytoid and lymphoma-like
  *…
related_trials.answers_question: Adjuvant RT plus immunotherapy combinations with non-overlapping toxicity
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 1 adjuvant concurrent IO + RT safety in bladder

papers.title: ASTRO 2025: Bladder Adjuvant Radiotherapy (BART): Clinical Outcomes from a Phase III Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
papers.abstract: ASTRO 2025 phase III Bladder Adjuvant Radiotherapy (BART), Advanced bladder cancer, cystectomy, advanced muscle invasive bladder cancer.
